title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, examining his career as a Florentine diplomat and his motivations for writing "The Prince" after exile. She draws parallels between Machiavelli's decision to restrict access to his political insights and Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR, arguing both saw their knowledge as posing existential dangers if misused.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer argues on Dwarkesh Patel's channel that Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time, reframing him through his career as a Florentine diplomat and his motivations for writing "The Prince" after exile. The central claim: Machiavelli chose to restrict access to his political insights because he saw that knowledge as dangerous if misused. Palmer draws a parallel to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR, arguing both men treated their knowledge as posing existential dangers.
